title: argyranthemum adauctum subsp. adauctum
intro: Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. adauctum, also known as the Canarian daisy or margalef daisy, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Canary Islands. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been employed to treat various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Argyranthemum adauctum subsp. adauctum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
